description: Enriched Sanskrit shlokas with proper transliterations and translations

shlokas.sanskrit: श्रीगणेशाय नमः। अथातो ब्रह्मजिज्ञासा।
shlokas.transliteration: Śrīgaṇeśāya namaḥ। Athāto brahmajijñāsā।
shlokas.translation: Salutations to Lord Ganesha. Now therefore, let us inquire about Brahman (the Supreme Reality).
shlokas.interpretation: The Hitopadesha begins with invoking divine wisdom. This teaches us to begin all endeavors with humility and seeking of true knowledge.

shlokas.sanskrit: मित्रभेदे तु कथयेत्संविदां कथिको जनः।
shlokas.transliteration: Mitrabhede tu kathayet saṁvidāṁ kathiko janaḥ।
shlokas.translation: In the matter of the Dissolution of Friendship, let the narrator speak.
shlokas.interpretation: This sets the stage for tales about how friendships break, teaching us about the fragility of relationships.

shlokas.sanskrit: सा विद्या या विमुक्तये विद्या च जीवनोपयोगिनी।
shlokas.transliteration: Sā vidyā yā vimuktaye vidyā ca jīvanopayoginī।
shlokas.translation: That is knowledge which leads to liberation, and also that which is useful in life.
shlokas.interpretation: True knowledge serves both spiritual liberation and practical living. This balances worldly and spiritual wisdom.

shlokas.sanskrit: आचारः परमो धर्मः।
shlokas.transliteration: Ācāraḥ paramo dharmaḥ।
shlokas.translation: Right conduct is the highest duty.
shlokas.interpretation: How we behave matters more than what we believe. Character is the foundation of dharma.

shlokas.sanskrit: न कश्चित्कस्यचिद्भेदं जानाति मनसः प्रभोः।
shlokas.transliteration: Na kaścit kasyacidbhedaṁ jānāti manasaḥ prabhoḥ।
shlokas.translation: No one knows the thoughts of another's mind.
shlokas.interpretation: We cannot truly know what others think. This teaches caution in trusting too deeply without evidence.

shlokas.sanskrit: धर्मार्थौ यत्र न स्यातामनृतं तत्र का गतिः।
shlokas.transliteration: Dharmārthau yatra na syātām-anṛtaṁ tatra kā gatiḥ।
shlokas.translation: Where dharma and wealth do not exist, and where falsehood prevails, what is the use of any place?
shlokas.interpretation: A just society requires both righteousness and prosperity. Without ethical foundations, progress is meaningless.

shlokas.sanskrit: विद्याधनं सर्वधनप्रधानं।
shlokas.transliteration: Vidyādhanaṁ sarvadhanapradhānaṁ।
shlokas.translation: Knowledge is the highest wealth.
shlokas.interpretation: Unlike material wealth, knowledge grows when shared and lasts forever. Invest in learning.

shlokas.sanskrit: अलसस्य कुतो धर्मो निधनं चाप्यलस्य किम्।
shlokas.transliteration: Alasya kutō dharmō nidhanaṁ cāpyalasya kim।
shlokas.translation: Of the lazy, what is dharma? And what is the use of the lazy one's death?
shlokas.interpretation: Lazy people cannot follow dharma properly, and their death has no purpose. Action and diligence are essential.

shlokas.sanskrit: उद्यमेन हि सिध्यन्ति कार्याणि न मनोरथैः।
shlokas.transliteration: Udayamena hi sidhyanti kāryāṇi na manorathaiḥ।
shlokas.translation: Works succeed through effort, not through mere desires.
shlokas.interpretation: Dreams remain dreams without action. Success requires deliberate effort and persistence.

shlokas.sanskrit: विरक्तस्यैव शर्मास्ति न विरक्तस्य दुःखितः।
shlokas.transliteration: Viraktasyaiva śarmāsti na viraktasya duḥkhitaḥ।
shlokas.translation: The detached alone has happiness; the attached alone has sorrow.
shlokas.interpretation: Attachment leads to suffering. Detachment brings peace. This applies to relationships, possessions, and outcomes.

shlokas.sanskrit: राजा सेव्योऽनुरक्तेन प्रजानां हितकाम्यया।
shlokas.transliteration: Rājā sevyo'nuraktena prajānāṁ hitakāmyayā।
shlokas.translation: The king should be served with devotion by those who seek the welfare of the people.
shlokas.interpretation: True leadership serves the people. Rulers must be devoted to the welfare of their subjects.

shlokas.sanskrit: विद्या विनयनीनां हि धनं दानप्रकाशिनाम्।
shlokas.transliteration: Vidyā vinayanīnāṁ hi dhanaṁ dānaprakāśinām।
shlokas.translation: Knowledge is the ornament of the learned; wealth is the ornament of the generous.
shlokas.interpretation: True education makes one humble. Wealth is meaningful when used generously.

shlokas.sanskrit: धर्मे सत्यं च लोकाश्च सर्वे धर्मं परायणात्।
shlokas.transliteration: Dharme satyaṁ ca lokāśca sarve dharmaṁ parāyaṇāt।
shlokas.translation: In dharma is truth, and all worlds rest on dharma.
shlokas.interpretation: Righteousness is the foundation of truth and the universe. Justice must be rooted in dharma.

shlokas.sanskrit: सत्यं ब्रूयात्प्रियं ब्रूयान्न ब्रूयात्सत्यं प्रियं।
shlokas.transliteration: Satyaṁ brūyāt priyaṁ brūyānna brūyāt satyaṁ priyam।
shlokas.translation: Speak truth, speak what is pleasant; do not speak pleasant untruth.
shlokas.interpretation: Balance honesty with kindness. Truth should be delivered with compassion.

shlokas.sanskrit: संगतः सज्जनैर्धर्म्यः संगतः पाखण्डिभिः कुसंगतः।
shlokas.transliteration: Saṁgataḥ sajjanoir-dharmyaḥ saṁgataḥ pākhaṇḍibhiḥ kusaṁgataḥ।
shlokas.translation: Association with the righteous leads to dharma; association with the wicked leads to ruin.
shlokas.interpretation: We become like those we associate with. Choose companions wisely.

shlokas.sanskrit: उत्तिष्ठत जाग्रत प्राप्नुत पराम्।
shlokas.transliteration: uttiṣṭhata jāgrata prāpnuta parām।
shlokas.translation: Arise, awake, and strive for the highest goal.
shlokas.interpretation: Sloth leads to ruin. We must be vigilant and work towards our highest purpose.

shlokas.sanskrit: नास्ति मित्रं परो रिपुर्नास्ति रिपुः परो मित्रं।
shlokas.transliteration: Nāsti mitraṁ paro ripurna-asti ripuḥ paraō mitraṁ।
shlokas.translation: There is no friend beyond an enemy, no enemy beyond a friend.
shlokas.interpretation: Enemies can become friends and friends can become enemies. Relationships are dynamic.

shlokas.sanskrit: कालः पर्याय इत्येके कालः परमेश्वरः।
shlokas.transliteration: Kālaḥ paryaya ityeke kālaḥ parameśvaraḥ।
shlokas.translation: Time is change, say some; Time is the Supreme Lord, say others.
shlokas.interpretation: Time is powerful and unstoppable. Work with time, not against it.

shlokas.sanskrit: मूर्खस्य परिज्ञानं न सुखं न च दुःखं।
shlokas.transliteration: Mūrkhasya pari jñānaṁ na sukhaṁ na ca duḥkham।
shlokas.translation: The recognition of a fool brings neither happiness nor sorrow.
shlokas.interpretation: Engaging with fools wastes time. Recognize and avoid such people.

shlokas.sanskrit: धनं ह्यवर्जनीयं धनहींनस्य न किञ्चन।
shlokas.transliteration: Dhanaṁ hyavarjanīyaṁ dhanahīnasya na kiñcana।
shlokas.translation: Wealth is indispensable; for one without wealth, there is nothing.
shlokas.interpretation: While not the highest value, wealth is necessary for a dignified life and to help others.

shlokas.sanskrit: मातृवत्परदारेषु परश्वात्र स्वजनेषु च।
shlokas.transliteration: Mātṛvat-paradāreṣu paraśvātra svajaneṣu ca।
shlokas.translation: Treat others' wives like your mother, others' wealth like sacred property, and all kindred as your own.
shlokas.interpretation: Respect for others' relationships and property is fundamental to social harmony.

shlokas.sanskrit: आत्मवत्परदुःखेन दुःखितः परश्च तत्परः।
shlokas.transliteration: Ātmavat-paraduḥkhena duḥkhitaḥ paraśca tatparaḥ।
shlokas.translation: He who causes pain to others suffers equally; the enemy of my enemy is my friend.
shlokas.interpretation: Harming others ultimately harms ourselves. Strategic alliances can overcome enemies.

shlokas.sanskrit: विद्या ददाति विनयं विनयाद्याति पात्रताम्।
shlokas.transliteration: Vidyā dadāti vinayaṁ vinayādyāti pātratām।
shlokas.translation: Knowledge gives humility; from humility, one gains worthiness.
shlokas.interpretation: True education makes us humble. Humility opens doors to wisdom and opportunity.

shlokas.sanskrit: राजा जानकीशस्त्रभृतां प्रभुः।
shlokas.transliteration: Rājā jānakīśastrabhṛtāṁ prabhuḥ।
shlokas.translation: The king is the master of those who bear arms.
shlokas.interpretation: Leadership requires the ability to protect. Power comes with responsibility.

shlokas.sanskrit: कालोऽहं चराचरस्य जीवितं च मृतं च मे।
shlokas.transliteration: Kālo'haṁ carācarasya jīvitaṁ ca mṛtaṁ ca me।
shlokas.translation: I am Time, the cause of life and death for all moving and non-moving beings.
shlokas.interpretation: Time is the ultimate force. Everything is subject to time's passage.

shlokas.sanskrit: न हि सुप्तस्य सिंहस्य प्रविशन्ति मृगां रजः।
shlokas.transliteration: Na hi suptasya siṃhasya praviśanti mṛgāṁ rajaḥ।
shlokas.translation: The dust does not enter the eyes of a sleeping lion.
shlokas.interpretation: The mighty need not fear small troubles. But remain vigilant even in strength.

shlokas.sanskrit: सखा साधुः समृद्धो यः सर्वकार्येषु साधकः।
shlokas.transliteration: Sakhā sādhuḥ samṛddho yaḥ sarvakāryeṣu sādhakaḥ।
shlokas.translation: A good friend is one who is virtuous, prosperous, and helpful in all endeavors.
shlokas.interpretation: True friends support our growth and stand by us in difficulties. Value such friendships.

shlokas.sanskrit: बुद्धिः पराक्रमश्लाघ्या विनीतस्य च विक्रमः।
shlokas.transliteration: Buddhiḥ parākramaślāghyā vinītasya ca vikramaḥ।
shlokas.translation: Wisdom is to be praised, and valor of the humble is praised.
shlokas.interpretation: Both intelligence and humble courage are virtues. Combine both for success.

shlokas.sanskrit: धर्मविद्भ्यः प्रदातव्यं न धर्मेभ्योऽन्यतः क्वचित्।
shlokas.transliteration: Dharmavidbhyaḥ pradātavyaṁ na dharmebhyo'nyataḥ kvacit।
shlokas.translation: Wealth should be given to the righteous, never to the unrighteous.
shlokas.interpretation: Support those who follow dharma. Resources given to the wicked cause harm.

shlokas.sanskrit: शुचिः सत्यवचाश्चारुर्दक्षो धर्मपरायणः।
shlokas.transliteration: Śuciḥ satyavacāścārur-dakṣo dharmaparāyaṇaḥ।
shlokas.translation: One who is pure, truthful, elegant, capable, and devoted to dharma.
shlokas.interpretation: These qualities define a noble person. Strive to develop all of them.

shlokas.sanskrit: कर्मण्येवाधिकारस्ते मा फलेषु कदाचन। मा कर्मफलहेतुर्भूर्मा ते सङ्गोऽस्त्वकर्मणि।।2.47।।
shlokas.transliteration: Karmanyeva adhikaras te ma phaleshu kadachana. Ma karmaphalaheturbhurna ma te sango 'stvakarmani.
shlokas.translation: You have the right to action alone, never to its fruits. Let not the fruits of action be your motive, nor let your attachment be to inaction.
shlokas.interpretation: This is the key teaching of Karma Yoga. Do your duty without attachment to results. This frees you from anxiety and makes you effective in action.

shlokas.sanskrit: योगस्थः कुरु कर्माणि सङ्गं त्यक्त्वा धनुः स्थितः। समत्वं योग उच्यते दुःखमाप्नोति पाण्डव।।2.48।।
shlokas.transliteration: Yogasthah kuru karmani sangam tyaktva dhanuh sthiritah. Samatvam yoga ucchayate duhkham apnoti pandava.
shlokas.translation: Be steadfast in yoga, O Arjuna. Perform your duty, abandoning attachment. Be impartial to success and failure. This equilibrium is called yoga.
shlokas.interpretation: True yoga is mental equilibrium. When you act without attachment, success and failure become equal. This brings peace even in the midst of action.

shlokas.sanskrit: न तु मां शक्यसे द्रष्टुमनेनैव स्वप्रभावा। दूरेण च रूपमादृश्यमनेनैव सर्वं जगत्।।2.14।।
shlokas.transliteration: Na tu mam shakyase drishtum anenaiva swaprabhava. Durecha rupam adrishtum anenaiva sarvam jagat.
shlokas.translation: But you cannot see Me in this form. I have no such form. All this world is pervaded by Me in My unmanifest form.
shlokas.interpretation: The appearances we see are temporary. The underlying reality is unseen but pervades everything. This teaches us to look beyond superficial appearances.

shlokas.sanskrit: यदा यदा हि धर्मस्य ग्लानिर्भवति भारत। अभ्युत्थानमधर्मस्य तदात्मानं सृजाम्यहं।।4.7।।
shlokas.transliteration: Yada yada hi dharmasya glanirbhavati bharata. Abhyutthanam adharmasya tadatmanam srijamyaham.
shlokas.translation: Whenever dharma declines and adharma rises, I manifest Myself. For the protection of the righteous and destruction of the wicked, I appear from age to age.
shlokas.interpretation: Divine intervention comes when righteousness is threatened. This gives hope that good will ultimately prevail over evil.

shlokas.sanskrit: परित्राणाय साधूनां विनाशाय च दुष्कृताम्। धर्मसंस्थापनार्थाय सम्भवामि युगे युगे।।4.8।।
shlokas.transliteration: Paritranaya sadhunam vinashaya cha duskritam. Dharma samsthapanarthaaya sambhavami yuge yuge.
shlokas.translation: To deliver the righteous, to destroy the wicked, and to re-establish dharma, I appear age after age.
shlokas.interpretation: The divine purpose is to protect the virtuous and punish the evil. This assures us that justice will be served.

shlokas.sanskrit: ज्ञेयः स नित्यसंन्यासी न कर्मफलहेतुर्भूः। न च सङ्गदेषु जायते।।5.3।।
shlokas.transliteration: Jneyah sa nitya sanyasi na karmaphalaheturbhuh. Na cha sangadesha jayate.
shlokas.translation: The renouncer who neither rejoices in worldly pleasures nor hates them is to be known as ever-established in unity. Free from dualities, he is easily liberated from material bondage.
shlokas.interpretation: True renunciation is mental detachment from both pleasure and pain. This state brings liberation from the cycle of suffering.

shlokas.sanskrit: उद्धरेदात्मनात्मानं नात्मानमवसादयेत्। आत्मैव ह्यात्मनो बन्धुरात्मैव रिपुरात्मनः।।6.5।।
shlokas.transliteration: Uddhared atmanatma na atmana mavasadayet. Atmaiva hyatmana bandhuratmaiva ripuratmanah.
shlokas.translation: Elevate yourself by the power of your mind, and do not degrade yourself. The mind can be your best friend or your worst enemy.
shlokas.interpretation: Our mind determines our destiny. We must control our thoughts to rise above difficulties. The mind is both our greatest asset and liability.

shlokas.sanskrit: बन्धुरात्मात्मनस्तस्य येनात्मैवात्मना जितः। अनात्मनस्तु शत्रुत्वे वर्तेात्मा शत्रुवत्समः।।6.6।।
shlokas.transliteration: Bandhuratma tmanas tena yena atmaivatmana jitah. Anatmana stu shatrutve varteta atma shatruvat samah.
shlokas.translation: For one who has conquered the mind, the mind is the best friend. But for one who has failed to do so, the mind remains the greatest enemy.
shlokas.interpretation: Self-mastery is the highest achievement. Those who control their minds find peace. Those who don't are troubled by their own thoughts.

shlokas.sanskrit: यो मां पश्यति सर्वत्र सर्वं च मयि पश्यति। तस्याहं न प्रणश्यामि स च मे न प्रणश्यति।।12.7।।
shlokas.transliteration: Yo mam pashyati sarvatra sarvam cha mayi pashyati. Tasya aham na pransyami sa cha me na pranshyati.
shlokas.translation: He who sees Me everywhere and sees everything in Me, to him I am never lost, nor is he ever lost to Me.
shlokas.interpretation: Devotion to the divine connects us permanently. The devotee and the divine are never separated.

shlokas.sanskrit: मां च योऽव्यभिचारेण भक्तियोगेन सेवते। स गुणान्मदतेऽतीतान्ब्रह्मभूयाय कल्पते।।14.26।।
shlokas.transliteration: Mam cha yo avyabhicarena bhaktiyogena sevate. Sa gunan matate atitan brahma bhuyaya kalpate.
shlokas.translation: And whoever serves Me with unwavering devotion, he transcends the three gunas and becomes qualified for realization of the Supreme.
shlokas.interpretation: Pure devotion transcends material qualities. By dedicating all actions to the divine, one rises above duality and attains spiritual realization.

shlokas.sanskrit: अभयं सत्त्वसंशुद्धिर्ज्ञानाग्निविरागदमः। शौच सत्यं क्षमा दानं दम इत्याभिचरणं प्रति।।16.1।।
shlokas.transliteration: Abhayam sattvasamsuddhir jnanagni viraga damah. Shaucha satyam kshama danam dam iti abhiharanam prati.
shlokas.translation: Fearlessness, purity of heart, steady practice of knowledge, charity, control of senses, offering of knowledge, study of scriptures, austerity, uprightness, non-violence, truthfulness, absence of anger, renunciation, peacefulness - these are the qualities of those born with divine virtues.
shlokas.interpretation: Divine qualities lead to liberation. Cultivating these virtues brings spiritual progress and peace.

shlokas.sanskrit: सर्वधर्मान्परित्यज्य मामेकं शरणं व्रज। अहं त्वा सर्वपापेभ्यो मोक्षयिष्यामि मा शुचः।।18.66।।
shlokas.transliteration: Sarva dharmaan parityajya mam ekam sharanam vraja. Aham tva sarva papebhyo moksayishyami ma shuchah.
shlokas.translation: Abandon all varieties of dharma and just surrender unto Me. I will deliver you from all sins. Do not grieve.
shlokas.interpretation: Ultimate surrender to the divine brings liberation from all sins. This is the simplest path to spiritual realization.
